questions.question: Define NP-hardness using the concept of reductions.
keyPoints: A problem is NP-hard if every problem in NP can be reduced to it in polynomial time
Does not require the problem to be in NP itself

questions.question: Outline the steps to prove that a problem B is NP-hard using reduction from known problem A.
keyPoints: 2. Construct polynomial-time transformation from A instances to B instances
3. Prove YES-instance ⇨ YES-instance and vice versa
4. Conclude B is at least as hard as A
